cwe: CWE-770 - Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling
CWE-920 - Improper Restriction of Power Consumption

threat.title: WebSocket Connection Exhaustion Attack
threat.description: Attackers exploit WebSocket servers that lack proper connection limits, rate limiting, and timeout configurations to exhaust server resources through connection flooding, slowloris-style attacks, or message bombing. By opening hundreds or thousands of persistent WebSocket connections and sending minimal data (or no data), attackers can consume all available server resources, preventing legitimate users from connecting and causing complete service disruption.

attack.scenario:
1. Reconnaissance: Attacker identifies WebSocket endpoint (e.g., wss://api.example.com/ws) through browser DevTools or API documentation
2. Connection Flooding: Attacker writes a script to open 10,000+ simultaneous WebSocket connections from distributed IP addresses
3. Slowloris Attack: Each connection sends minimal data (e.g., one byte every 60 seconds) to keep connections alive without triggering timeouts
4. Resource Exhaustion: Server exhausts available file descriptors, memory, and thread pool resources
5. Service Degradation: Legitimate users unable to establish new connections; existing connections experience timeouts
6. Complete Outage: Server becomes unresponsive; monitoring systems detect 100% connection pool utilization and application crashes
prerequisites: WebSocket endpoint accessible without authentication OR weak authentication
Missing or insufficient rate limiting on connection establishment
No message size limits or message rate throttling
Lack of idle connection timeout or heartbeat mechanisms
Insufficient server resource allocation (connection pool size, memory)

cve_references.description: Apache Tomcat WebSocket DoS via incomplete cleanup during closure. Affects Tomcat 11.0.0-M1 to 11.0.0-M16, 10.1.0-M1 to 10.1.18, 9.0.0-M1 to 9.0.85, 8.5.0 to 8.5.98
Denial of Service in ws library when number of received headers exceeds server.maxHeadersCount threshold
IBM MQ vulnerable to Slowloris DoS attack due to improper enforcement of timeout on individual read operations
Apache HTTP Server: WebSocket protocol upgrades over HTTP/2 cause NULL pointer dereference leading to server crash
Azure IoT TLS Listener Slowloris DoS: malicious user can open 6.4 TcpStreams per second with 0 bytes, triggering DoS

real_world_breaches.impact: Major service disruption affecting millions of users globally for several hours
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Unusual traffic spikes causing WebSocket connection exhaustion and service degradation

real_world_breaches.impact: Proof-of-concept demonstrated complete service disruption of real-time gaming platforms through connection exhaustion
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Lack of per-IP connection limits allowed single attacker to exhaust connection pools

real_world_breaches.impact: Multiple chat applications vulnerable to message flooding attacks causing memory exhaustion
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Missing message rate limiting and size restrictions on WebSocket frames

bug_bounty_reports.summary: WebSocket connection exhaustion allowing complete service disruption through unlimited connection establishment

mitigations.title: Implement Per-IP Connection Limits with Express.js ws Library
mitigations.description: Enforce strict per-IP connection limits to prevent any single client from exhausting server resources. This mitigation tracks active connections by IP address and rejects new connections when limits are exceeded.
iso27001_controls: A.17.2.1 - Availability of information processing facilities
nist_csf: PR.PT-5 - Mechanisms are implemented to achieve resilience
PR.DS-4 - Adequate capacity to ensure availability is maintained
DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
validation_steps: Test connection limit by opening MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP + 1 connections from same IP
Verify idle connections close after CONNECTION_TIMEOUT seconds
Send messages exceeding MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE and verify rejection
Flood with MESSAGE_RATE_LIMIT + 10 messages and verify throttling
Monitor server resources during stress test with 1000+ connections

mitigations.title: Flask-SocketIO Rate Limiting and Connection Management
mitigations.description: Implement comprehensive rate limiting for Flask-SocketIO applications including per-IP connection limits, message rate throttling, and automatic connection cleanup for idle clients.

code_example.code:
from flask import Flask, request
from flask_socketio import SocketIO, emit, disconnect
from functools import wraps
import time
from collections import defaultdict
from threading import Lock

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SECRET_KEY'] = 'your-secret-key-from-env'
socketio = SocketIO(app, cors_allowed_origins="https://yourdomain.com")

# Configuration
MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP = 10
MESSAGE_RATE_WINDOW = 60  # seconds
MAX_MESSAGES_PER_WINDOW = 100
MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE = 64 * 1024  # 64KB
IDLE_TIMEOUT = 60  # seconds

# Connection tracking
connections_by_ip = defaultdict(list)
connection_lock = Lock()
message_counts = defaultdict(lambda: {'count': 0, 'reset_time': time.time() + MESSAGE_RATE_WINDOW})
last_activity = {}

def get_client_ip():
    """Extract client IP from request"""
    if request.headers.get('X-Forwarded-For'):
        return request.headers.get('X-Forwarded-For').split(',')[0].strip()
    return request.remote_addr

def check_connection_limit():
    """Check if client has exceeded connection limit"""
    client_ip = get_client_ip()
    with connection_lock:
        if len(connections_by_ip[client_ip]) >= MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP:
            return False
    return True

def rate_limit(event_name):
    """Decorator for rate limiting SocketIO events"""
    def decorator(f):
        @wraps(f)
        def wrapped(*args, **kwargs):
            client_ip = get_client_ip()
            sid = request.sid
            key = f"{client_ip}-{sid}"
            
            now = time.time()
            client_data = message_counts[key]
            
            # Reset counter if window expired
            if now > client_data['reset_time']:
                client_data['count'] = 0
                client_data['reset_time'] = now + MESSAGE_RATE_WINDOW
            
            # Check rate limit
            if client_data['count'] >= MAX_MESSAGES_PER_WINDOW:
                emit('error', {
                    'message': 'Rate limit exceeded',
                    'retry_after': int(client_data['reset_time'] - now)
                })
                return
            
            client_data['count'] += 1
            last_activity[sid] = now
            
            return f(*args, **kwargs)
        return wrapped
    return decorator

def check_message_size(data):
    """Validate message size"""
    if isinstance(data, str):
        size = len(data.encode('utf-8'))
    elif isinstance(data, bytes):
        size = len(data)
    else:
        size = len(str(data).encode('utf-8'))
    
    return size <= MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE

@socketio.on('connect')
def handle_connect():
    """Handle new WebSocket connections with rate limiting"""
    client_ip = get_client_ip()
    sid = request.sid
    
    # Check connection limit
    if not check_connection_limit():
        print(f"Connection limit exceeded for IP: {client_ip}")
        return False  # Reject connection
    
    # Track connection
    with connection_lock:
        connections_by_ip[client_ip].append(sid)
    
    last_activity[sid] = time.time()
    
    print(f"Client connected: {client_ip} (SID: {sid})")
    print(f"Active connections from {client_ip}: {len(connections_by_ip[client_ip])}/{MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP}")
    
    emit('connection_success', {'message': 'Connected successfully'})

@socketio.on('disconnect')
def handle_disconnect():
    """Handle disconnection and cleanup"""
    client_ip = get_client_ip()
    sid = request.sid
    
    # Remove from tracking
    with connection_lock:
        if sid in connections_by_ip[client_ip]:
            connections_by_ip[client_ip].remove(sid)
            if not connections_by_ip[client_ip]:
                del connections_by_ip[client_ip]
    
    # Cleanup activity tracking
    if sid in last_activity:
        del last_activity[sid]
    
    # Cleanup message counts
    key = f"{client_ip}-{sid}"
    if key in message_counts:
        del message_counts[key]
    
    print(f"Client disconnected: {client_ip} (SID: {sid})")

@socketio.on('message')
@rate_limit('message')
def handle_message(data):
    """Handle incoming messages with rate limiting and size validation"""
    # Validate message size
    if not check_message_size(data):
        emit('error', {'message': f'Message too large. Max size: {MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE} bytes'})
        return
    
    # Process message
    try:
        # Your message processing logic here
        print(f"Received message from {request.sid}: {data[:100]}...")  # Log first 100 chars
        emit('message_received', {'status': 'success', 'timestamp': time.time()})
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error processing message: {e}")
        emit('error', {'message': 'Failed to process message'})

@socketio.on('ping')
@rate_limit('ping')
def handle_ping():
    """Handle ping messages for keepalive"""
    last_activity[request.sid] = time.time()
    emit('pong', {'timestamp': time.time()})

def check_idle_connections():
    """Background task to close idle connections"""
    while True:
        socketio.sleep(30)  # Check every 30 seconds
        now = time.time()
        
        idle_connections = [
            sid for sid, last_time in last_activity.items()
            if now - last_time > IDLE_TIMEOUT
        ]
        
        for sid in idle_connections:
            print(f"Closing idle connection: {sid}")
            socketio.emit('idle_timeout', 
                         {'message': 'Connection closed due to inactivity'},
                         room=sid)
            disconnect(sid=sid)

# Start background task for idle connection cleanup
socketio.start_background_task(check_idle_connections)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    print(f"Starting Flask-SocketIO server...")
    print(f"Max connections per IP: {MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP}")
    print(f"Max messages per {MESSAGE_RATE_WINDOW}s: {MAX_MESSAGES_PER_WINDOW}")
    print(f"Idle timeout: {IDLE_TIMEOUT}s")
    socketio.run(app, host='0.0.0.0', port=5000, debug=False)

nist_csf: PR.PT-5 - Mechanisms are implemented to achieve resilience
validation_steps: Test connection limit by spawning MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP + 1 clients
Verify rate limiting triggers after MAX_MESSAGES_PER_WINDOW messages
Send message exceeding MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE and verify rejection
Test idle timeout by creating connection with no activity
Monitor memory usage during high connection load

mitigations.title: Infrastructure-Level DoS Protection with Azure Front Door and WAF
mitigations.description: Implement cloud-native DDoS protection and Web Application Firewall rules to detect and mitigate connection flooding attacks before they reach application servers.

code_example.code:
#!/bin/bash
# Azure Front Door and DDoS Protection Configuration

# Variables
RESOURCE_GROUP="myResourceGroup"
LOCATION="eastus"
FRONTDOOR_NAME="myFrontDoor"
WAF_POLICY_NAME="myWAFPolicy"
BACKEND_ADDRESS="myapp.azurewebsites.net"

# Create WAF Policy
az network front-door waf-policy create \
  --resource-group $RESOURCE_GROUP \
  --name $WAF_POLICY_NAME \
  --mode Prevention \
  --disabled false

# Configure rate limiting rule (10,000 requests per minute per IP)
az network front-door waf-policy rule create \
  --policy-name $WAF_POLICY_NAME \
  --resource-group $RESOURCE_GROUP \
  --name RateLimitRule \
  --rule-type RateLimitRule \
  --action Block \
  --priority 100 \
  --rate-limit-duration-in-minutes 1 \
  --rate-limit-threshold 10000

# Add custom rule to block excessive WebSocket connection attempts
az network front-door waf-policy rule create \
  --policy-name $WAF_POLICY_NAME \
  --resource-group $RESOURCE_GROUP \
  --name WebSocketConnectionLimit \
  --rule-type MatchRule \
  --action Block \
  --priority 200 \
  --match-variable RequestHeader \
  --operator Equal \
  --match-values "websocket" \
  --transforms Lowercase \
  --selector "Upgrade"

# Enable DDoS Protection Standard
az network ddos-protection create \
  --resource-group $RESOURCE_GROUP \
  --name myDDoSProtection \
  --location $LOCATION

# Create Front Door profile
az afd profile create \
  --resource-group $RESOURCE_GROUP \
  --profile-name $FRONTDOOR_NAME \
  --sku Premium_AzureFrontDoor

# Associate WAF policy with Front Door
az afd security-policy create \
  --resource-group $RESOURCE_GROUP \
  --profile-name $FRONTDOOR_NAME \
  --security-policy-name mySecurityPolicy \
  --domains myendpoint.azurefd.net \
  --waf-policy $WAF_POLICY_NAME

# Configure custom health probe for WebSocket endpoints
az afd probe create \
  --resource-group $RESOURCE_GROUP \
  --profile-name $FRONTDOOR_NAME \
  --probe-name websocket-probe \
  --path "/health" \
  --probe-request-type GET \
  --interval-in-seconds 30

# Enable diagnostic logs for monitoring
az monitor diagnostic-settings create \
  --resource "/subscriptions/{subscription-id}/resourceGroups/$RESOURCE_GROUP/providers/Microsoft.Network/frontDoors/$FRONTDOOR_NAME" \
  --name FrontDoorDiagnostics \
  --logs '[{"category": "FrontdoorAccessLog", "enabled": true}, 
          {"category": "FrontdoorWebApplicationFirewallLog", "enabled": true}]' \
  --workspace "/subscriptions/{subscription-id}/resourceGroups/$RESOURCE_GROUP/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/myLogAnalytics"

echo "Azure Front Door and WAF configuration complete"
echo "Rate limiting: 10,000 requests/minute per IP"
echo "DDoS Protection: Enabled"
echo "WAF Mode: Prevention"

iso27001_controls: A.17.2.1 - Availability of information processing facilities
nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
DE.AE-1 - A baseline of network operations is established
validation_steps: Verify WAF policy is in Prevention mode
Test rate limiting with load testing tool (e.g., Artillery, K6)
Review WAF logs in Azure Monitor for blocked requests
Simulate connection flood and verify automatic mitigation
Configure alerts for DDoS attack detection

indicators: Sudden spike in WebSocket connection attempts from limited IP ranges
High number of connections with minimal or no message activity
Server CPU and memory usage increases without proportional user activity
Connection pool exhaustion warnings in application logs
Increase in connection timeouts and failed handshakes for legitimate users
Slowloris pattern: many connections sending data at very slow rate (1 byte per minute)
Single IP address with MAX_CONNECTIONS or more active connections
Message rate significantly below normal while connections remain open
Geographic anomalies: connections from unusual countries/regions

log_queries.query:
// Detect WebSocket connection exhaustion patterns
let threshold = 50; // connections per IP
AzureDiagnostics
| where ResourceType == "FRONTDOOR" or ResourceType == "APPLICATIONGATEWAY"
| where requestUri_s contains "/ws" or requestUri_s contains "websocket"
| extend ClientIP = coalesce(clientIP_s, remoteIP_s)
| summarize 
    ConnectionCount = count(),
    UniqueEndpoints = dcount(requestUri_s),
    AvgResponseTime = avg(timeTaken_s),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by ClientIP, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where ConnectionCount > threshold
| extend Duration = datetime_diff('minute', LastSeen, FirstSeen)
| where Duration > 5 // Sustained attack for 5+ minutes
| project TimeGenerated, ClientIP, ConnectionCount, Duration, UniqueEndpoints, AvgResponseTime
| order by ConnectionCount desc

index=websocket_logs sourcetype=ws_access
| eval connection_time=_time
| stats 
    count as connections,
    earliest(connection_time) as first_connection,
    latest(connection_time) as last_connection,
    dc(session_id) as unique_sessions,
    sum(message_count) as total_messages,
    avg(message_count) as avg_messages
    by client_ip
| eval duration_minutes = (last_connection - first_connection) / 60
| eval messages_per_connection = total_messages / connections
| where connections > 50 
    AND messages_per_connection < 5 
    AND duration_minutes > 5
| eval severity = case(
    connections > 200, "critical",
    connections > 100, "high",
    connections > 50, "medium"
  )
| table client_ip, connections, duration_minutes, messages_per_connection, severity
| sort - connections

// WebSocket DoS detection
event.dataset: "websocket.access" AND event.action: "connection"
| stats 
    count() as connection_count,
    cardinality(session.id) as unique_sessions,
    sum(websocket.message_count) as total_messages,
    min(@timestamp) as first_seen,
    max(@timestamp) as last_seen
    by source.ip
| where connection_count > 50
| eval messages_per_connection = total_messages / connection_count
| where messages_per_connection < 5
| eval duration = (last_seen - first_seen) / 60000
| where duration > 5
| sort connection_count desc

fields @timestamp, sourceIP, @message
| filter ispresent(websocket.connectionId)
| stats 
    count() as connectionCount,
    count_distinct(websocket.connectionId) as uniqueSessions,
    sum(websocket.messageCount) as totalMessages,
    min(@timestamp) as firstSeen,
    max(@timestamp) as lastSeen
    by sourceIP
| filter connectionCount > 50
| fields sourceIP, connectionCount, uniqueSessions, 
    (totalMessages / connectionCount) as messagesPerConnection,
    ((lastSeen - firstSeen) / 60000) as durationMinutes
| filter messagesPerConnection < 5 and durationMinutes > 5
| sort connectionCount desc
